Scenario.overview: Transportation Systems Complex A sophisticated cyber attack targeting transportation systems involves multiple stages, starting with reconnaissance to identify network infrastructure, followed by resource development to establish a foothold, and culminating in impact through disrupting operations.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Network Traffic, User Account, Application Log, Target Artifact (Interact), Mobile device, Logon Session, Channel
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Delivery Method, Network Traffic Flow (Mechanism), Response Metadata (Mechanism), Bash Script, Web Shell, Application Log Content (Mechanism), User Account Authentication (Mechanism), Network Service Scanning, Module Load (Mechanism)

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 309; Name: Network Topology Mapping
stage.description: The attackers conduct active scanning to map out the network topology of the transportation systems, identifying potential entry points and vulnerabilities.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 522; Name: Malicious Hardware Component Replacement
stage.description: Adversaries setup phishing infrastructure using free email services to prepare for credential harvesting campaigns.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2020-25079 (D-Link DCS-2530L and DCS-2670L devices contains a command injection vulnerability)
stage.description: Employees receive phishing emails containing login credentials harvesting links, leading to unauthorized access via exploited vulnerabilities in internet-facing assets.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2024-9680 (Mozilla Firefox and Firefox ESR use-after-free vulnerability)
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 88; Name: OS Command Injection
stage.description: Once initial access is gained, adversaries execute malicious scripts using command-line interfaces to exploit identified vulnerabilities.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 650; Name: Upload a Web Shell to a Web Server
stage.description: A web shell is installed on a server to maintain persistent access to the compromised environment.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 121; Name: Exploit Non-Production Interfaces
stage.description: Attackers find and exploit known vulnerabilities to escalate privileges within the targeted applications.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 578; Name: Disable Security Software
stage.description: Security tools such as antivirus software are impaired to avoid detection by the attackers.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 49; Name: Password Brute Forcing
stage.description: Repeated attempts are made to guess passwords, aiming to gain unauthorized access to user accounts.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 309; Name: Network Topology Mapping
stage.description: Attackers scan the network to discover additional systems and services for lateral movement opportunities.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.description: Stolen credentials are used to move laterally across the network, gaining access to new systems.

stage.name: Collection
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 277; Name: Data Interchange Protocol Manipulation
stage.description: Sensitive data related to the operation of the transport systems is collected from repositories.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 24; Name: Filter Failure through Buffer Overflow
stage.description: Collected data is transferred to attacker-controlled servers over a command and control channel.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 276; Name: Inter-component Protocol Manipulation
stage.description: Operational technology is disrupted to cause damage, impacting the functionality of the transportation system, akin to the Maroochy Water Breach.

Scenario.summary: The complex cyber attack on the transportation systems involved extensive reconnaissance, resource development, and exploitation of multiple vulnerabilities to achieve persistent access and disrupt critical operations, mirroring the tactics seen in historical incidents like the Maroochy Water Breach.
